page: Amazon Web Services c6id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores with hyper-threading, utilizing the x86_64 architecture on an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local storage directly attached to the hypervisor. Networking capabilities feature a baseline bandwidth of 19 Gbps. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions, and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA extensions.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6id.12xlarge demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads. It achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48,783.94 and an encryption test score of 43,659.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 41.34 GB/sec. For small language model inference using a 135M model, it processes prompts at 2,456.37 tokens/second and generates text at 306.31 tokens/second. Other metrics, such as Geekbench multi-core and Redis SET operations, perform in the average range.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2 and integrated high-capacity NVMe SSD storage, the c6id.12xlarge offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring both high CPU performance and fast local scratch space. It is well-suited for applications such as high-performance web serving, software compilation, image processing, cryptographic tasks, and database operations that benefit from direct NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance demonstrates strong benchmark performance, particularly in static web serving (41.34 GB/sec), CPU encryption, and image processing, alongside solid capabilities in small-scale LLM inference. With its 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-speed local storage, the c6id.12xlarge is optimized for compute-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and cryptographic applications requiring fast local read/write speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong performance for web serving and cryptographic workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and strong cryptographic and web-serving performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 vCPUs on a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor
Equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong performance in static web serving and cryptographic operations
Features AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Optimized for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, and database operations
